geopolitics_summary: EU-China trade tensions escalating; G7 reaffirms Russia sanctions; US-Iran oil deal uncertain; geopolitical risk premium supports crude and gas.

themes.headline: EU greenhouse gas emissions rose in 2025, stagnating climate progress
themes.why_it_matters: Rising EU emissions signal weaker carbon abatement trajectory, pressuring EUA prices downward and reducing carbon floor under power costs; impacts Cal+1 forward valuations.

themes.headline: Data center lobby warns EU must choose between AI and climate goals
themes.why_it_matters: Push for gas-fired peaker capacity to support AI load growth directly increases gas demand and power prices; tension between climate policy and grid reliability supports thermal generation investment.

themes.headline: G7 reaffirms Ukraine support and Russian sanctions; Trump hints oil sanctions
themes.why_it_matters: Potential resumed US oil sanctions on Russia tightens crude and LNG global supply, raising EU gas LNG arb spreads and TTF price floor; indirect support for power via fuel-switching economics.

themes.headline: Permian natural gas production rose 60% from 2021 to 2025; rising LNG export capacity
themes.why_it_matters: Surge in US LNG supply (27.6 Bcf/d) increases availability for EU arbitrage; higher LNG inflows to Northwest Europe weaken TTF curve and reduce power-gas spread, pressuring EU power prices.

themes.headline: US-Iran oil deal uncertain to ease Europe's energy security pressures
themes.why_it_matters: If deal fails, loss of Iranian barrels sustains crude risk premium and narrows LNG-to-EU arb, keeping gas import costs elevated; if succeeds, marginal pressure on TTF via reduced crude-oil scarcity.

watchlist: European Council summit Thu 19 Jun: watch trade/China and sanctions policy statements for crude-gas arb impact.
G7 oil sanctions on Russia: monitor Trump administration follow-through; direct TTF tail-risk if implemented.
EU emissions data trend: track Q3 2026 EUA auction clearing prices and forward curve steepening.
Permian LNG export ramp: watch for capacity announcements and US Gulf ship-loading delays affecting NW Europe arbitrage.
